questions.question: Who among the following was bestowed with the title of Saheb-e-Alam Bahadur by Bahadur Shah during the uprising of 1857?
questions.explanation: Bakht Khan was a Pashtun related to the family of Rohilla Chief Najib-ud-daula. Bakht Khan arrived in Delhi on 1July 1857 which improve the leadership position of BahadurShah Zafar. Bakht Khan’s superior abilities quickly became evident and the emperor gave him actual authority and title of ‘Saheb-e-Alam- Bahadur.

questions.question: Which Indian art form ispracticed in the Mithila region of Bihar and Nepal?
questions.explanation: Madhubani Painting is practiced in the Mithila region of Bihar and Nepal. It is known for its intricate patterns, vibrant colours, and depiction of nature, folklore, and religious themes. Kalamezhuthu is an ancient art woven into the ritualistic mores of Bhagavati, Naga and Ayyappa temples in Kerala. Thanjavur painting is a classical South Indian painting style. Pattachitra is a general term for traditional, cloth-based scroll painting, based in the eastern Indian states of Odisha.

questions.question: Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi came back to India in the year _____ after living abroad for two decades?
questions.explanation: Gandhiji lived in Africa for about 21 years. He returned to India on 9 January 1915. Gandhiji’s first important public speech was in February 1916 at opening ceremony of the Banaras Hindu University. His political mentor was Gopal Krishna Gokhale.

questions.question: Which festival marks the victory of Goddess Durga over the demon king Mahishasura?
questions.explanation: The word Navratri means 'Nine Nights'. During these time, new forms of Shakti are worshipped Navratri festival marks the victory of Goddess Durga over the demon king Mahishasura. Navratri is celebrated twice a year in shardiya and chaitra.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs is associated with the taxation system of the Marathas?
questions.explanation: The taxation system of the Marathas was primarily based on a system known as the "Chauth" and "Sardeshmukhi". a) Chauth: It was a land revenue tax collected from territories under Maratha control, which was 25% of the land revenue collected by the local rulers. b)Sardeshmukhi: This was an additional 10% tax imposed on the territories outside the Maratha kingdom, collected from areas under Maratha influence.

questions.question: In which state of India is the Seshachalam Biosphere Reserve located?
questions.explanation: The Seshachalam Biosphere Reserve is situated in the state of Andhra Pradesh. It was designated as a Biosphere Reserve in 2010 and is spread across the Chittor and Kadapa districts. This reserve is part of the Eastern Ghats and is known for its unique flora and fauna. The reserve encompasses Tirupati, amajor Hindu Pilgrimage town, and Sri Venkateswara National Park.

questions.question: Which one of the following temple complexes has a massive statue of Nandi, considered to be the biggest in India?
questions.explanation: The Lepakshi temple, also known as Veerbhadra temple is located in the tiny hamlet of Anantapur District of Andhra Pradesh. It is a paragon of excellent architecture and art. It is famous as it has largest Nandi.

questions.question: Which British physicist was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in1906 for his work on discovery of electrons?
questions.explanation: J. J. Thomson was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1906 for his work on discovery of electrons. James Chadwick discovered neutrons. Niels Bohr proposed a model of the atom. Ernest Rutherford postulated the nuclear structure of the atom, discovered alpha and beta rays, and proposed the laws of radioactive decay.

questions.question: Hyderabad is situated on the banks of which of the following river?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad is situated on the banks of the Musi River. The Musi River flows through the city of Hyderabad, which is in the Indian state of Telangana. The river originates from the Anantagiri Hills, located in the Vikarabad district, and runs through the city before merging with the Krishna River. The Musi River has played a significant role in the development of Hyderabad, historically influencing the city's urbanization and providing water for agriculture and domestic use. The city’s famous landmarks, including the Charminar and the Salar Jung Museum, are located near the Musi River.

questions.question: What is the mean temperature of Earth [In degree Celsius]?
questions.explanation: The mean temperature of Earth is 16 degree Celsius [61F]. El-Aziziyan region of Libya with maximum temperature of 58 degree Celsius is the warmest place whereas Vostok in Antarctica is the coldest place on the earth with temperature reaching as low as –87.5 degree Celsius.

questions.question: Which form of farming is called 'Slash and Burn' Agriculture?
questions.explanation: Primitive subsistence farming is called as slash and burn agriculture. Farmers clear a patch of land and produce cereals and other food crops to sustain their families. When the soil fertility decreases, the farmers shift and clear a fresh patch of land for cultivation. Slash and burn farming is known by different names in various parts of the world. Some of the common alternative names are: 1. Swidden Agriculture: A common term used, especially in tropical regions. 2. Shifting Cultivation: This term highlights the practice of moving from one plot of land to another after the soil loses its fertility. 3. Jhum Farming: Used in Northeastern India, particularly in the states of Assam, Nagaland, and Mizoram. 4. Milpa Farming: A term used in Central America, especially in Mexico, referring to the traditional slash-and-burn farming method. 5. Kaingin Farming: Used in the Philippines to describe the slash-and-burn technique.

questions.question: Which Country Won ‘2024 Under-19 Cricket World Cup’ Title?
questions.explanation: Host Country: South Africa Champions: Australia [4th title] Runner up- India Player of the series: Kwena Maphaka [South Africa] Australia won the 2024 Under-19 Cricket World Cup by defeating India by 79 runs in the final.

questions.question: GROW report and portal, recently seen in the news, is launched by which one of the following?
questions.explanation: The GROW report and portal, recently featured in the news, were launched by NITI Aayog, the National Institution for Transforming India. The initiative, titled "Greening and Restoration of Wasteland with Agro forestry [GROW]", was unveiled on February 12, 2024, by Professor Ramesh Chand, a member of NITI Aayog. This multi-institutional effort employs remote sensing and Geographic Information Systems [GIS] to assess agro forestry suitability across all districts in India. The GROW portal provides universal access to state and district-level data, facilitating the restoration of degraded lands through agro forestry practices. The primary objectives of the GROW initiative include restoring 26 million hectares of degraded land by 2030 and creating an additional carbon sink of 2.5 to 3 billion tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent.

questions.question: Which ministry recently inaugurated the ‘ASSOCHAM Environment and Carbon Conference’ in New Delhi?
options: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
questions.explanation: Union minister of state for Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Kirti Vardhan Singh inaugurated the ASSOCHAM Environment and Carbon Conference in New Delhi. The conference theme is “Fostering a collaborative ecosystem for achieving net zero by 2070.” The government aims to achieve net zero emissions by 2070 and fulfill the Vikshit Bharat 2047 vision. He highlighted the success of the Jal Jeevan Mission in solving water crises for rural farmers. The government supports industries and stakeholders in addressing environmental issues. The conference includes representatives from industries, research, and environmental organizations.

questions.question: Tejas Light Combat Aircraft, recently seen in the news, is developed by which organization?
questions.explanation: Tejas, a 4.5-generation single-seat supersonic multirole fighter, was developed by DRDO’s Aeronautical Development Agency and HAL. Intended to replace older MiG-21 and Su-7 jets, it joined the IAF in 2016. It has three models and is known for being the smallest and lightest in its class, with versatile combat roles.

questions.question: How many articles were originally included in the Indian Constitution at the time of its implementation?
questions.explanation: The total number of articles that were present originally in the Indian Constitution during its implementation is 395 in 22 parts and eight schedules. As of January 2025, the Indian Constitution has been amended 106 times, containing 448 articles, 25 parts, and 12 schedules.

questions.question: In India, who among the following has the power to pardon, remit or commute the punishment of a criminal?
questions.explanation: In India, the President of India has the power to pardon, remit, or commute the punishment of a criminal. This power is granted under Article 72 of the Indian Constitution, which allows the President to grant pardons, respites, or to reduce the severity of punishment in certain cases, particularly for those convicted of offenses against the law of the Union.

questions.question: Which of the following organs are NOT parts of the Alimentary canal?
questions.explanation: The alimentary canal, also known as the digestive tract, is a long tube that runs from the mouth to the anus. It includes several organs that help in the process of digestion and absorption of nutrients. The organs that are part of the alimentary canal include: a) Mouth b) Esophagus c) Stomach d) Small intestine e) Large intestine [colon] f) Rectum g) Anus These organs form the pathway through which food travels, and digestion occurs. However, accessory organs of digestion, like the liver, pancreas, and gallbladder, are not part of the alimentary canal, although they aid in digestion by secreting digestive enzymes and bile.

questions.question: Which of the following programmes was launched on 15th August, 1983 with the objective of providing guaranteed employment for about 100 days in a year to at least one member of landless families?
options: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act [MGNREGA]
Training Rural youth for self Employment [TRYSEM]
questions.explanation: Rural-landless Employment Guarantee Programme [RLEGP] was launched on 15th August, 1983 with the objective of providing guaranteed employment for about 100 days in a year to at least one member of landless families. It was later integrated with other schemes and evolved into the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act [MGNREGA] in 2005, which aimed to provide wage employment to rural households.

questions.question: Macro economics deals with?
options: the decision making of a single economic variable like demand
questions.explanation: Macro economics is the study of aggregate economy. It focuses on National output, unemployment, and inflation. It deals with circular flow of income

questions.question: What is the common name of the compound ????????????????????₃?
questions.explanation: The common name of the compound 𝑁𝑎𝐻𝐶𝑂₃ is Baking Soda. Baking soda, also known as sodium bicarbonate [NaHCO₃], is a white, crystalline powder commonly used in baking as a leavening agent. It releases carbon dioxide gas when mixed with an acid, causing dough or batter to rise. Apart from its culinary uses, baking soda is also used for cleaning, deodorizing, and as a mild antacid. Na₂CO₃ is the chemical formula for sodium carbonate, which is commonly known as soda ash or washing soda. It is a white, odourless, water-soluble salt that is widely used in various industries such as glass manufacturing, detergents, and chemicals. Bleaching powder, also known as calcium hypochlorite, has the chemical formula Ca[OCl]₂. It is a chemical compound commonly used for disinfecting, bleaching, and purifying water. It is a white, granular powder with a strong odour of chlorine and is widely used in the textile, paper, and cleaning industries, as well as for water treatment. Caustic soda, also known as sodium hydroxide [NaOH], is a highly caustic and alkaline compound. It is a white, odourless solid that is highly soluble in water, releasing heat in the process. Caustic soda is widely used in industries such as soap manufacturing, water treatment, paper production, and chemical processing. It is also used as a drain cleaner and in various chemical reactions.

questions.question: On what basis is the Modern Periodic Table organized?
questions.explanation: The Modern Periodic Table is based on the Atomic Number of the elements. Dmitri Mendeleev is known as the father of the Modern Periodic Table. At present, 118 elements are known to us. All these have different properties. Out of these 118, only 94 are naturally occurring.

questions.question: Why do stars twinkle in the sky at night?
options: Due to atmospheric refraction of Starlight
questions.explanation: Stars twinkle in the sky at night due to atmospheric refraction of starlight. Examples of refraction are -twinkling of stars in a clear sky, pool of water appears to be less deep than what it actually is, rainbow formation in the sky, camera lenses, glasses, water droplets.

questions.question: Which part of the cell converts nutrients into energy?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria, often referred to as the ''Power house of the cell", are organelles that convert the energy stored in nutrients into ATP [Adenosine Triphosphate], which is used by the cell as a source of chemical energy. This process is known as cellular respiration, where glucose and other nutrients are broken down and transformed into usable energy for the cell.

questions.question: Which of the following is a UNESCO recognised dance form?
questions.explanation: Among the following Kalbelia the folk dance form of Rajasthan is recognised by UNESCO, while others are not. UNESCO has recognized several traditional dance forms from India as Intangible Cultural Heritage. Some of the major UNESCO-recognized dance forms from India include: a) Bharatanatyam [Tamil Nadu] b) Kathak [North India] c) Kathakali [Kerala] d) Kuchipudi [Andhra Pradesh] e) Manipuri [Manipur] f) Odissi [Odisha]

[Correct to two decimal place]
questions.explanation: Marks of Anil in all subject = 65 + 75 + 90 + 65 + 77 = 372 Marks of Sumit in all subjects = 82 + 95 + 55 + 85 + 74 = 391 {391 – 372}/391 × 100% Less = 4.86% Less

questions.question: Let O be the centre of the circle and PQ and RS are two parallel chords on the different side of the centre. OA is perpendicular to PQ and OB is perpendicular to RS. If PQ=30cm, RS= 16cmand AB= 23cm, then find the diameter of the circle. [in cm]

questions.question: Which of the following can be the value of ‘P’ so that the number 9536P is divisible by 6?
questions.explanation: 9536 P; P can be 0, 2, 4, 6, 8 Sum of the number = 9 + 5 + 3 + 6 + P = 23 + P [Divisible by 3] P → 4

questions.question: The pie chart given below shows the production [percentage] of car by company ‘X’ in different year. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997042364bd36.08362432.png) If total number of car produced in 2018, and 2019 together was 3960, then find the number of cars produced by the company in 2021 and 2022 together?

Pipe P, Q and R fill a tank in 20, 30 and 40 minute respectively. Initially pipes P and R are kept open for 10 minutes and then pipe P is closed while pipe Q is opened. Pipe R is closed 5 minute before the tank overflows. How much time [in minute] will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: P → 20 {6} → 120 Q → 30 {4} → 120 R → 40 {3} → 120 {6 + 3} × 10 = 90 units Remaining = 120 - 90 = 30 R is closed 5 min before the tank overflow: R.W. = 30 + 5 × 3 = 45 units This work will be done by Q and R Time taken = 45/{4 + 3} = 6 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704238b6547.67283708.gif) minutes Total time taken = 16 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970423dfe567.01139684.gif) minutes

questions.question: If x² varies inversely to y and x=4, when y=2, then find the value of y when x =6.
questions.explanation: x² ∝ {1/y} x² = k/y y = 2, then x = 4 16 = k/2, and k = 32 x² = 32/y when x = 6, y = 32/36 = 8/9

questions.question: If a sum of Rs. 15000 is taken at a single rate of simple interest of 12% per annum for 2 years and another sum of Rs. 9000 is taken at a simple interest of 10% per annum for 4 years find the difference of the interest paid.
questions.explanation: 1st sum interest = 15000 × 24% = 3600 2nd interest = 9000 × 40% = 3600 Difference = 0

questions.question: A train of length 220m is running at a speed of x km/hr crosses a bridge of length of 140m in 12 sec. Find the value of x {in km/hr}.
What is the volume of a cylinder if the radius of the cylinder is 10 cm and height is 1.4 times of the radius of the cylinder?
questions.explanation: Radius = 10 cm, so, height = 14 cm Volume of cylinder = πr²h Volume = π × 10 × 10 × 14 = 4400 cm³

questions.question: If 2x-y=z, then which of the following expressions is true?
questions.explanation: 2x - y = z {x – y}² = {z– x}² x² - 2xy + y² = z² - 2xz + x² y² + 2xz = z² + 2xy

questions.question: What is the perimeter of the sector of a circle with a radius of 14cm and an angle 45°?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970424bb1fe1.83861800.png) Arc = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970424d7d117.14268340.gif) × 2 × ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970424e8fce9.09607881.gif) × 14 = 11 Perimeter = 2 × 14 + 11 = 39 cm

questions.question: How many years will it take for Rs. 16000 to amount Rs. 18522 at a compound interest rate of 5% per annum when compounded annually?
If a+b is 10% more than a-b, then a is how much % more or less than b?

If diagonals of a quadrilateral is equal and all the vertex angles are bisected by the diagonals, then the quadrilateral is called _______________ >यदि किसी चतुर्भुज के विकर्ण बराबर हों तथा सभी शीर्ष कोण विकर्णों द्वारा समद्विभाजित हों, तो चतुर्भुज को _______________ कहा जाता है।
questions.explanation: If diagonals of a quadrilateral is equal and all the vertex angles are bisected by the diagonals, then the quadrilateral is a Square. > यदि किसी चतुर्भुज के विकर्ण बराबर हों तथा सभी शीर्ष कोण विकर्णों द्वारा समद्विभाजित हों, तो चतुर्भुज एक वर्ग होता है।

questions.question: Rohan purchased an item for which there was a 13.5% discount offered on the part of the payment made in cash and a 6.25% surcharge on the part of the payment made through a credit card. If Rohan would have paid the entire amount in cash, he would have paid Rs y for the item. But he actually had to pay Rs. 40964 in all for the item, out of which Rs. 29064 was paid in cash. What is the value of y [in Rs.]?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970425369133.56510513.png)

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: Use Adapt THEMSELVES to. CONCEPT: Use reflexive pronoun after following verbs - acquit, avail, reconcile, amuse, resign, avenge, exert, apply, adapt, adjust, pride, absent and enjoy etc. The verb "adapt" implies a change or modification in one's behaviour, attitude, or way of life, and the reflexive pronoun emphasizes that this change is being made by the individuals themselves. The correct sentence would be: "Wherever they go, Indians easily adapt themselves to local circumstances."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Serious threat to our ecology and environment can be ________ with organic cultivation.
questions.explanation: The word "combated" means to fight or oppose something, especially a problem or an evil. In this context, the sentence suggests that serious threats to our ecology and environment can be fought or opposed with organic cultivation. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Aggravated - बढ़ाया हुआ - Made something worse. Example- "The situation was aggravated by the lack of communication." • Combated - लड़ा गया - Fought or opposed something. Example- "The team combated the problem with a new strategy." • Hastened - तेज किया - Made something happen sooner. Example- "The arrival of the new equipment hastened the completion of the project." • Impeded - रोका गया - Slowed or hindered something. Example- "The construction was impeded by the bad weather." Hence, Option B - COMBATED, is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. His brother in laws who have come to meet him live in the USA.
options: His brothers in law who has come
His brother in laws who has come
His brothers in law who have come
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: His brothers in law who have come….. • The term "brother-in-law" is a compound noun. When forming the plural, only the primary noun ("brother") takes the plural form, not the entire phrase. Hence, the correct plural form is "brothers-in-law". • The verb "have" is used correctly here, as it agrees with the plural subject "brothers-in-law." • The sentence refers to multiple brothers-in-law who have come, so "brothers" must be plural, and the rest of the sentence remains unchanged. Improved sentence: "His brothers-in-law who have come to meet him live in the USA."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: A number of students lacks willpower that plays a crucial role in determining the result of one’s efforts.
A number of students lack willpower that plays a crucial role in determining the result of one’s efforts.
A number of student lack willpower that play a crucial role in determining the result of one’s efforts.
A number of student lack willpower that plays a crucial role in determining the result of one’s efforts.
questions.explanation: Explanation - The sentence describes a situation where many students are being referred to, and their lack of willpower is noted as important. The grammatical correctness of the sentence depends on subject-verb agreement and correct use of noun. • The phrase "A number of" indicates plurality, so the subject "students" is plural. The plural verb "lack" correctly agrees with the plural subject "students." • The relative clause "that plays a crucial role" refers to "willpower," which is singular. The singular verb "plays" is therefore correct. Thus, Option B is the only grammatically correct sentence - A number of students lack willpower that plays a crucial role in determining the result of one’s efforts.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: Yacht is the correct spelling. Yacht - याट - A large, luxurious boat used for recreation or entertainment.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The prime minister is ill hence we will have to put away meeting for a few days.
questions.explanation: The correct phrase to describe the postponement of a meeting due to the prime minister being ill is "put off", which means to delay or defer something to a later time. Meaning of other options: • Put on: This means to wear something or to start an event or performance. Example: "She put on a coat before going outside." • Put of: This is not a correct English phrase and is likely a typographical error. • Put away: This means to store or save something in its proper place, or in some contexts, to imprison someone. Example: "He put away the groceries in the fridge." Hence, Option C is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - I felt a great affinity with the people of the Highlands.
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Affinity - लगाव - A natural liking or understanding of someone or something. Example: "She has an affinity for classical music." • Disparity - असमानता - A great difference or inequality. Example: "The disparity between the rich and the poor is increasing." • Greed - लालच - An intense desire for something, especially wealth or power. Example: "His greed for money led him to make poor choices." • Empathy - सहानुभूति - The ability to understand and share the feelings of another. Example: "Her empathy for others makes her a great counsellor." • Aversion - अरुचि - A strong dislike or disinclination. Example: "He has an aversion to spicy food." Hence, EMPATHY is the most appropriate synonym of AFFINITY as both refer to feelings of understanding, connection, or a bond with others.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. JOVIAL
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Jovial - खुशमिजाज - Cheerful, friendly, and full of joy. Example: "His jovial personality made everyone feel comfortable at the party." • Cunning - चतुर/धूर्त - Skilled at achieving one's goals through deceit or cleverness. Example: "The cunning fox escaped the trap easily." • Gloomy - उदास - Sad, dark, or pessimistic in mood or atmosphere. Example: "The gloomy weather dampened their spirits." • Sly - चालाक - Clever or deceitful, often in a sneaky way. Example: "The sly thief managed to avoid detection." • Assertive - आत्मविश्वासी - Confident and forceful in expressing oneself. Example: "She was assertive during the debate, making her points clear and strong." Hence, GLOOMY is the most appropriate antonym of JOVIAL because while "jovial" refers to cheerfulness and happiness, "gloomy" conveys sadness or a lack of joy.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. My grandfather cannot part ________ his money.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "My grandfather cannot part WITH his money." The phrase "part with" means to give up or let go of something, often reluctantly. In this context, it implies that the grandfather is unwilling to let go of his money.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. To take to one’s heels
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Option [D] - Run away in fear Explanation: “To take to one’s heels” – "डरकर भाग जाना या तेजी से भाग जाना" This idiom means to run away quickly, usually out of fear or panic. It conveys the act of fleeing from a situation. Origin of this Idiom : The phrase originates from the idea of someone turning and running so fast that only their heels are visible as they flee. It has been used since the 16th century to describe swift escape. Example: "When the thief saw the police approaching, he took to his heels and disappeared into the alley." Hindi translation: "जब चोर ने पुलिस को पास आते देखा, तो वह डर के मारे भाग खड़ा हुआ।"

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The corrected sentence would be: "How long have you been learning English for?" CONCEPT: The error is in the tense usage. The phrase "are you learning" uses the present continuous tense, which is incorrect in this context because the question asks about an activity that started in the past and is continuing in the present. The correct tense to express this ongoing action is the present perfect continuous tense, which uses "have been" followed by the verb in its present participle form ["learning"].

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. CAPRICIOUS
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Capricious - मनमौजी या चंचल - Unpredictable, impulsive, or prone to sudden changes in mood or behavior. Example: "Her capricious decisions made it difficult for others to rely on her." Now, let's analyze the given options: • Whimsical - विचित्र या चंचल - Playful, unpredictable, or prone to sudden ideas or changes, often in a fanciful way. Example: "The whimsical artist constantly shifted between different styles." • Courteous - विनम्र - Polite, respectful, and considerate in behavior. Example: "He was always courteous to his guests." • Rude - अशिष्ट - Impolite or lacking manners. Example: "His rude comments upset everyone in the room." • Reasonable - तर्कसंगत - Logical, fair, or sensible. Example: "The proposal was reasonable and gained everyone’s support." Hence, WHIMSICAL is he most appropriate synonym of CAPRICIOUS.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- I detest any form of cruelty toward animals.
questions.explanation: Detest – "घृणा करना" – To strongly dislike or hate something. Example: "I detest dishonesty in any form." Now, let's analyze the given options: • Eliminate - समाप्त करना – To completely remove or get rid of something. Example: "We need to eliminate waste to keep the environment clean." • Defend - बचाव करना – To protect or support something. Example: "She defended her brother against the accusations." • Adore - प्रेम करना – To love deeply or have a great admiration for something. Example: "She adores spending time with her pets." • Profess - घोषणा करना – To declare or claim something openly. Example: "He professed his innocence during the trial." Hence, ADORE is the most appropriate antonym of DETEST.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. I will ________ that the car arrives by six o'clock.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "I will ENSURE that the car arrives by six o'clock." This sentence means that the speaker is taking responsibility to make certain or guarantee that the car will arrive on time. Here are the explanations: • Ensure - सुनिश्चित करना - To make certain or guarantee something. Example: "The teacher will ensure that all students understand the lesson." • Insure - बीमा करना - To provide insurance for something, typically to protect against financial loss. Example: "He will insure his car against theft." • Assure - आश्वासन देना - To give confidence to someone or reassure them about something. Example: "I assure you that everything will be fine." • Insecure – असुरक्षित - not confident or certain about something. Example: She felt insecure about her appearance.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Ordinary and dull
questions.explanation: Here are the explanations for all options: • Menace - खतरा - A person or thing that is likely to cause harm or danger, Example: "The criminal was a menace to society." • Timid - डरपोक - Showing a lack of courage or confidence, Example: "The timid child hesitated to speak in front of the class." • Orifice - छिद्र - An opening, particularly one in the body, Example: "The orifice of the pipe was clogged with debris." • Mundane - साधारण और नीरस - Lacking excitement or interest; dull or ordinary, Example: "Her job involved mundane tasks like filing papers and answering emails." Hence, Mundane is the correct answer as it means "Ordinary and dull."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. No silver bullet can make the world safe from terrorism.
options: A simple and magical solution to a complex problem
questions.explanation: SILVER BULLET - एक जादुई समाधान - refers to a simple and magical solution to a complex or difficult problem. It suggests that certain problems are too intricate to be solved with a single, effortless method. Origin of this Idiom: The phrase "silver bullet" originates from folklore, where it was believed that only a silver bullet could kill mythical creatures like werewolves. This made it a symbol of a perfect and unique solution. Example: "There is no silver bullet to improve the economy; it requires structural reforms and consistent efforts." Explanation: This means that improving the economy is a complex process that cannot be achieved with one simple action but requires sustained, collective efforts over time. Hindi translation: "अर्थव्यवस्था को सुधारने के लिए कोई जादुई समाधान नहीं है; इसके लिए संरचनात्मक सुधार और निरंतर प्रयासों की आवश्यकता है।"

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Indifference to pleasure and pain
questions.explanation: Here are the words with their Hindi and English meanings, along with usage: • Snob - अभिमानी व्यक्ति - A person who feels superior to others based on taste, wealth, or social class, Example - "She refused to associate with her neighbors, acting like a complete snob." • Stoicism - सुख-दुख के प्रति उदासीनता - Indifference to both pleasure and pain, showing emotional resilience, Example - "He faced his financial struggles with stoicism, never complaining about his situation." • Hedonist - सुखवादी - A person who seeks pleasure and enjoyment as the primary goal of life, Example - "The hedonist spent his weekends indulging in lavish parties and fine dining." • Dormant - सुप्त - Inactive or in a state of rest, Example - "The project remained dormant for months due to a lack of funding." Hence, Stoicism is the correct answer as it means Indifference to pleasure and pain.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. Frequent elections makes it a mockery at B. The hands of our representatives C. India is a democratic country but D. Who play political games at the cost of the nation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: CABD This order makes sense because: • Sentence C introduces the topic by stating that India is a democratic country, setting the stage for the discussion. • Sentence A highlights the issue of frequent elections, which creates a mockery of democracy and connects to the theme introduced in Sentence C. • Sentence B elaborates on how this affects the democratic process, specifically putting power in the hands of representatives. • Sentence D concludes by pointing out the consequences, where representatives prioritize political games over the nation's welfare. Final paragraph: "India is a democratic country, but frequent elections make it a mockery at the hands of our representatives, who play political games at the cost of the nation."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Mr. Sharma was cleaning the house.
options: The house was cleaned by Mr. Sharma.
The house will be cleaned by Mr. Sharma.
The house is being cleaned by Mr. Sharma.
The house was being cleaned by Mr. Sharma.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The house was being cleaned by Mr. Sharma. Active voice structure: Mr. Sharma [Sub] + was cleaning [V] + the house [Obj]. Passive voice structure: The house [Obj] + was being cleaned [V3] + by Mr. Sharma [by + Sub]. This option correctly changes the voice of the sentence from active to passive, emphasizing the object [the house] rather than the subject [Mr. Sharma]. The verb tense remains consistent in the past continuous tense with "was being cleaned."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He said , “Children, drink water.”
options: He said that Children drink water.
He said that Children should drink water.
He advised the Children to drink water.
He said that Children drank water.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: He advised the Children to drink water. Explanation: In indirect speech, imperative sentences [like commands, requests, or advice] are reported using verbs such as "advised," "requested," or "ordered," depending on the context. The verb "said" is replaced with "advised" to convey the sense of suggestion or guidance.

questions.question: We wake up to notifications, check them before we drink water, before we register the weight of the day ahead. The glow of the screen sets the tone. A like, a comment, a message, we begin the morning _____1_____our worth in metrics. We post, we scroll, we compare. The feed is endless. So _____2_____ the need to keep up. It did not start this way. Social media promised connection, the easy intimacy of a shared moment, the ability to reach _____3______ time zones, continents. Logging _____4_____ feels like falling behind. We tell ourselves we are in control. That we could stop anytime. We talk about digital detoxes the way we talk about diets, something we _____5_____ to start on Monday. But the truth is, stepping away feels like erasure. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The word "measuring" fits perfectly in the context, implying that people assess their self-worth based on likes, comments, and messages on social media. Let’s see the other options: - "Scintillating" means attractive or impressive, which doesn't convey the idea of assessing self-worth. - "Exposing" means revealing or making something visible, which doesn't fit the context. - "Allocating" means assigning or distributing resources, which also doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: We wake up to notifications, check them before we drink water, before we register the weight of the day ahead. The glow of the screen sets the tone. A like, a comment, a message, we begin the morning _____1_____our worth in metrics. We post, we scroll, we compare. The feed is endless. So _____2_____ the need to keep up. It did not start this way. Social media promised connection, the easy intimacy of a shared moment, the ability to reach _____3______ time zones, continents. Logging _____4_____ feels like falling behind. We tell ourselves we are in control. That we could stop anytime. We talk about digital detoxes the way we talk about diets, something we _____5_____ to start on Monday. But the truth is, stepping away feels like erasure. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The sentence should read: "So is the need to keep up." The verb "is” agrees with the subject "need" and indicates a state of being, implying that the need to keep up is ongoing and constant. Using "is" creates a sense of timelessness and universality, implying that the need to keep up is a persistent and enduring aspect of our lives. Let’s see other options: - "Will" implies a future tense, which doesn't fit the context. - "Has" implies a past tense or possession, which doesn't fit the context. - "Were" implies a past tense or subjunctive mood, which doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: We wake up to notifications, check them before we drink water, before we register the weight of the day ahead. The glow of the screen sets the tone. A like, a comment, a message, we begin the morning _____1_____our worth in metrics. We post, we scroll, we compare. The feed is endless. So _____2_____ the need to keep up. It did not start this way. Social media promised connection, the easy intimacy of a shared moment, the ability to reach _____3______ time zones, continents. Logging _____4_____ feels like falling behind. We tell ourselves we are in control. That we could stop anytime. We talk about digital detoxes the way we talk about diets, something we _____5_____ to start on Monday. But the truth is, stepping away feels like erasure. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The preposition "across" indicates extension or movement from one side to the other, often implying a sense of breadth or scope. In this context, "across" suggests the ability to connect with people or places separated by large distances, such as different time zones or continents. Let’s see other options: - "Through" implies movement from one end to the other, often with a sense of passage or progression. - "Above" implies a sense of superiority or elevation, which doesn't fit the context. - "Amidst" implies a sense of being surrounded or situated in the middle of something, which also doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: We wake up to notifications, check them before we drink water, before we register the weight of the day ahead. The glow of the screen sets the tone. A like, a comment, a message, we begin the morning _____1_____our worth in metrics. We post, we scroll, we compare. The feed is endless. So _____2_____ the need to keep up. It did not start this way. Social media promised connection, the easy intimacy of a shared moment, the ability to reach _____3______ time zones, continents. Logging _____4_____ feels like falling behind. We tell ourselves we are in control. That we could stop anytime. We talk about digital detoxes the way we talk about diets, something we _____5_____ to start on Monday. But the truth is, stepping away feels like erasure. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The phrase "logging off" means disconnecting or signing out of a computer system, network, or online platform. In this context, it implies that taking a break from social media or disconnecting from it feels like falling behind or missing out.

questions.question: We wake up to notifications, check them before we drink water, before we register the weight of the day ahead. The glow of the screen sets the tone. A like, a comment, a message, we begin the morning _____1_____our worth in metrics. We post, we scroll, we compare. The feed is endless. So _____2_____ the need to keep up. It did not start this way. Social media promised connection, the easy intimacy of a shared moment, the ability to reach _____3______ time zones, continents. Logging _____4_____ feels like falling behind. We tell ourselves we are in control. That we could stop anytime. We talk about digital detoxes the way we talk about diets, something we _____5_____ to start on Monday. But the truth is, stepping away feels like erasure. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The verb "intend" means to plan or mean to do something, often implying a sense of purpose or resolution. In this context, it fits perfectly with the idea of planning to start a digital detox or a diet on Monday. Let’s see other options - - "Expand" means to increase or grow something, which doesn't fit the context. - "Demand" means to require or insist on something, which also doesn't fit the context. - "Spend" means to use or allocate time, money, or resources, which doesn't fit the context either.
